title: Semiquincentennial Congressional Time Capsule Act
plain_summary: This bill creates a special time capsule to celebrate America's 250th birthday in 2026. The time capsule will be buried on the west lawn of the Capitol building by July 4, 2026. Congressional leaders will choose what items to put inside the time capsule. The time capsule will stay buried and sealed until July 4, 2276, when it can be opened 250 years later.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Semiquincentennial Congressional Time Capsule Act. The word semiquincentennial means the two hundred and fiftieth anniversary of something.

sections.title: Section 2 Semiquincentennial Congressional Time Capsule
sections.text:
The person in charge of maintaining the Capitol building (called the Architect of the Capitol) must create a time capsule. This time capsule will be called the Semiquincentennial Congressional Time Capsule.

The top leaders from both political parties in the House and Senate will decide together what goes inside the time capsule. These leaders are the Speaker of the House, the Minority Leader of the House, the Majority Leader of the Senate, and the Minority Leader of the Senate.

The time capsule must contain several types of items. It must include books, documents, printed materials, souvenirs, historical objects, and other things related to America's two hundred and fiftieth birthday celebration. It must also include copies of important laws and major events that happened in Congress before the time capsule is buried. The time capsule must contain a message from today's Congress to the future Congress that will open it. The leaders can add other items they think are important.

When making these decisions, the congressional leaders can ask for advice from the Architect of the Capitol, the head of the Smithsonian Institution (a group of museums), and other government agencies.

The Architect of the Capitol has two main jobs. First, the Architect must prepare the time capsule to be sealed and buried on the West Lawn of the Capitol building by July fourth, two thousand and twenty six. The burial must happen at a time that allows people to attend both this event and another time capsule burial happening in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania on the same day. Second, the Architect must put up a sign or plaque that gives information about the time capsule.

The time capsule will stay buried and sealed until July fourth, two thousand two hundred and seventy six. On that date, the Speaker of the House will give the time capsule to the Congress that is serving then, which will be the two hundred and forty fourth Congress. That future Congress will decide how to preserve or use what is inside the time capsule.
